title: Allowing non-root users to modify resources copied to an image is security-sensitive
description: Allowing non-root users to modify resources copied to an image is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotdockerfilecweOwnership or write permissions for a file or directory copied to the Docker image have been assigned to a user other than root.Write permissions enable malicious actors, who have a foothold on the container, to tamper with the resource and thus potentially manipulate the container’s expected behavior.Manipulating files could disrupt services or aid in escalating privileges inside the container.This also breaches the container immutability principle as it facilitates container changes during its life. Immutability, a container best practice, allows for a more reliable and reproducible behavior of Docker containers.If a user is gi
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Using --chmod with write permissions for group or others is security-sensitive. Non-root users can modify resources, which violates container immutability and allows potential tampering. Use --chmod with restrictive permissions (e.g., 644, 755) or --chown to set ownership without granting write access.
